title: Wedge bounce: the sole that saves your bunker shots
microFact: Bounce is the angle that stops your wedge digging. For most golfers and most Indian conditions, medium bounce (10–12°) is the safe answer.
lesson:
Look at a wedge's sole: the trailing edge hangs LOWER than the leading edge. That angle is BOUNCE, and it's what makes the club skid through turf and sand instead of digging in like a shovel.

• LOW bounce (4–6°): for firm, tight turf and shallow sweepers. Versatile for opening the face, but dig-prone.
• MID bounce (7–12°): the all-rounder. If unsure, buy this.
• HIGH bounce (13°+): for soft/fluffy sand and steep diggers. Bunker-friendly.

High-handicap reality: most beginners hit slightly BEHIND the ball. Bounce is your insurance — the club skips forward instead of stabbing the ground, and the shot survives. That's why a mid-to-high bounce sand wedge (56° with 10–12° of bounce) is the standard prescription, and why low-bounce lob wedges wreck beginners.

Shop fluency: bounce is stamped on the wedge (e.g., "56.12" = 56° loft, 12° bounce). Ask "what bounce is this?" and watch the salesperson recalibrate their respect.

visual.caption: Bounce: the trailing edge hangs below the leading edge, so the sole skids instead of digging.

quiz.question: What does '56.12' stamped on a wedge mean?
quiz.explanation: Loft dot bounce is the standard stamp. 56° of loft with 12° of bounce — a forgiving sand wedge spec.
